channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Kazakhstan's Emergency Response System Revolutionizes Public Safety
item.description: Kazakhstan's SOS 102 app sets a new standard for public safety with its omni-channel design, AI-powered filtering, and real-time tracking features.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: SOS 102: A new era in emergency response for Kazakhstan
item.description: Emergency response systems worldwide are undergoing rapid transformation, and Kazakhstan is no exception. With the introduction of the SOS 102 app, the country has set a new standard for public safety, redefining how law enforcement interacts with citizens.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Canon's new RF 16-28mm F2.8 wide-angle zoom lens impressed me, but I'm less convinced we need it
item.description: The new Canon RF 16-28mm F2.8 IS STM ultra-wide zoom lens is a smaller and cheaper alternative to Canon's pro L-series lenses, ideal for enthusiast cameras like the EOS R8.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Musk clashes with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman over Trump-supported Stargate AI data center project
item.description: Elon Musk is clashing with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman over the Stargate artificial intelligence infrastructure project touted by President Donald Trump, the latest in a feud between the two tech billionaires that started on OpenAI's board and is now testing Musk's influence with the new president.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Will Samsung's new Galaxy AI features come to older devices? Here's what we know
item.description: Samsung unveiled a slew of Galaxy AI features for its new Galaxy S25 lineup, but how many of them are likely to trickle down to older handsets?
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Stratoshark: A Game-Changing Tool for Cloud Operations Visibility
item.description: Sysdig's Stratoshark applies the Wireshark user interface and workflow to system-level data, allowing users to analyze system calls, inter-process communication, networking, command execution, and user activity in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Facebook’s group moderators are its last, best hope
item.description: By now, you may be sick of reading hot takes on Mark Zuckerberg’s decision to end Meta’s formal fact-checking activities, loosen its properties’ restrictions on hate speech, and retool itself for the Trump 2.0 era. So I promise I’m not going to burden you with yet another one.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Wisconsin man accused of setting fire to congressman's office over TikTok ban charged with arson
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Prosecutors charged a Wisconsin man who allegedly told police he tried to burn down a congressman's office because he was upset with the federal TikTok ban with multiple counts on Wednesday, including arson.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump pardons Ross Ulbricht, Texas-born founder of dark web marketplace website
item.description: President Donald Trump has pardoned Ross Ulbricht, the founder of Silk Road, an underground website for selling drugs.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Samsung aims to turn its next generation of Galaxy smartphones into AI companions
item.description: Samsung is injecting another dose of artificial intelligence into the next version of its Galaxy smartphones, escalating an effort to simplify people’s lives while deepening their dependence on a device that accompanies them almost everywhere.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Are you an educator or student? Google's new features for Chromebooks and more will make your life way easier
item.description: Google continues to enhance ChromeOS and Chromebook with even more useful accessibility and educational tools.
item.dc:creator: allisa.james@futurenet.com (Allisa James), TechRadar

item.title: Trump administration shuts down White House Spanish-language page and social media
item.description: Within hours of President Donald Trump’s inauguration, the new administration took down the Spanish-language version of the official White House website. The site — currently https://www.whitehouse.gov/es/ — now gives users an “Error 404” message.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Everything new on Max in February 2025
item.description: It's a new month, and Max's February 2025 schedule proves that the platform isn't playing around when it comes to new titles. Here's everything new on Max in February 2025.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia RTX 5090 and 5080 GPU stock shortage rumors are piling high – and now there’s a suggestion the RTX 5070 could be delayed
item.description: Fresh Nvidia next-gen GPU fears emerge: shaky stock, pricing worries, and a claim the RTX 5070 is delayed until March.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Why Trump dashed Biden’s AI executive order, and why it matters
item.description: During President Donald Trump’s first few hours in power, he revoked Joe Biden’s 2023 executive order on AI. In the absence of congressional action, Biden’s EO served as the first effort by the federal government to address the considerable societal and national security risks presented by AI.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Extreme cold and snow across the South isn't a threat to most native plants and animals, experts say
item.description: A deep freeze and rare snow have brought most things to a standstill from parts of Texas to Florida, but experts say wildlife natives like cypress trees and prairie plants, and cold-blooded gators and snakes, should be alright.
item.dc:creator: Corey Williams, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cloud infrastructure spending more than doubles in the third quarter of 2024
item.description: Spending on compute and storage infrastructure for cloud deployments has surged to unprecedented heights, with 115.3% year-over-year increase in the third quarter of 2024.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 5 new features in EDB Postgres AI
item.description: More than half of enterprises (56%) run mission-critical workloads in hybrid environments, according to a 2024 EDB survey of enterprise executives.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: From devops to CTO: 5 things to start doing now
item.description: I was promoted to CTO in my late twenties, and while it is common to see young CTOs leading startups these days, it was unusual in the ’90s. I was far less experienced back then, and still developing my business acumen.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to stop robocalls on your flip phone
item.description: Flip phones are harder to block spam calls from, but it's not impossible. Kurt the "CyberGuy" explains how to stop incoming robocalls on your flip phone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: TikTok app restored in US amid Trump assurances
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump plans to help the popular social media app TikTok stay in operation under his presidency.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that's probably because it has, at least if you're measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: DAVID HAMILTON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: TikTok goes dark as US ban takes effect
item.description: Millions of TikTok users in the United States are no longer able to watch videos on the social media platform as a federal ban on the immensely popular app takes effect.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he will ‘most likely’ give TikTok 90-day extension
item.description: “The 90-day extension is something that will be most likely done, because it's appropriate," Trump said in a call with NBC News.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Node.js set to stabilize type stripping
item.description: Node.js, the popular JavaScript runtime, is moving to stabilize type stripping, a feature that allows developers to execute TypeScript files without source maps or additional configuration, speeding up the development process. The Node.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: That Apple ID disabled message? It's a dangerous scam
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy breaks down why those emails that warn that "Your Apple ID has been disabled" is most likely a scam. Here's how to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: For TikTok users, mourning, frustration and clinging to hope as TikTok ban looms
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. is inching closer and closer to a potential TikTok ban — with the nation's highest court upholding a law that's set to halt new downloads of the app starting Sunday.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and MAE ANDERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Who could buy TikTok? Only a few parties are serious about offering
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The supremely popular TikTok could be banned on Jan. 19 under a federal law that forces the video sharing platform to divest itself from its China-based parent company, ByteDance, or shut down its U.S. operations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: These mistakes could tank your credit score
item.description: Even small mistakes can negatively impact your credit score; here are some of the most common credit report errors and how you can fix them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden's new executive order aims to shore up US cyber defenses
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden issued an executive order Thursday aimed at strengthening the nation's cybersecurity and making it easier to go after foreign adversaries or hacking groups that try to compromise U.S. internet and telecommunication systems.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC posts 57% surge in profit thanks to the AI boom
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Taiwan computer chip maker TSMC reported Thursday that its profit in the last quarter rose 57%, buoyed by the artificial intelligence boom. The world’s biggest semiconductor manufacturer reported a net profit of 374.7 billion new Taiwan dollars ($11.4 billion), higher than expected.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden cyber EO seeks proof of security from software vendors
item.description: The new cybersecurity executive order seeks to put teeth behind the secure software development standards that federal vendors are supposed to be following. The post Biden cyber EO seeks proof of security from software vendors first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network
